questions.question: In `shopify/ajax_load.php`, what determines whether the session-based or token-based initialization path is used?

options.text: The `$_REQUEST['auth_mode']` parameter
The presence of an `Authorization` header in `getallheaders()`
The `$_SESSION['auth_type']` variable
The `accountId` parameter value

questions.question: What does `get_current_account($accountId, $active_lp)` in `shopify/functions.php` return?

options.text: Only the `TBL_SP_ACCOUNTS` record for the given account ID
A merged object of `TBL_SP_LOGISTIC` + `TBL_USER_LOCATIONS` + `TBL_SP_ACCOUNTS` data
An array of all active Shopify accounts
The Shopify API connection object

questions.question: In the `orders.php` UI, what differentiates a "Pending" order from a "New" order?

options.text: Pending orders have a higher priority flag
Pending orders either have `dispatchAfterDate` in the future OR `hold='1'`; New orders are `status='new'` with no hold
Pending orders await payment confirmation; New orders have paid
Pending orders are from manual import; New orders are from webhooks

questions.question: How is the logistic partner class dynamically loaded in `ajax_load.php`?

options.text: Via `require_once(ROOT_PATH . '/includes/logistic/' . $active_lp . '.php')`
Via `include_once(ROOT_PATH . '/includes/class-' . $active_lp . '-client.php')` where `$active_lp` is derived from `strtolower($account->lp_provider_name)`
Through a factory pattern in `LogisticFactory::create($active_lp)`
Via the `shopify_dashboard` connector's `loadLogistic()` method

questions.question: In the `save_scan_ship` action, what happens per-UID after the operator submits scanned items?

options.text: Only `TBL_SP_ORDERS.uid` is updated with the UID array
`$stockist->update_inventory_status($uid, 'sales')` + `$stockist->add_inventory_log($uid, 'sales', 'Shopify Sales :: orderItemId')` + `TBL_PROCESS_LOG` insert
The UID is directly written to `TBL_INVENTORY.inv_status='shipped'`
`$stockist->update_stock_on_mps()` is called to decrement marketplace stock

questions.question: When `$stockist->update_inventory_status($uid, 'sales')` is called during scan & ship, what does the `before_update_inventory` trigger do?

options.text: Sends a notification to the warehouse manager
Updates the product's committed_stock counter
Clears `box_id`, `ctn_id`, `expectedLocation`, `currentLocation`, and `inv_batch` to NULL because `sales` is a terminal status
Creates a log entry in `TBL_INVENTORY_LOG`

questions.question: What inventory status must a UID have before it can be scanned during the scan & ship process?

options.text: `qc_pending` — item must be in QC queue
`inbound` — item must be received but not processed
`qc_verified` — only fully QC-passed items can be shipped
`sales` — item must already be allocated to the order

questions.question: What happens to `TBL_PRODUCTS_STOCK_RESERVATION` when the scan & ship action completes successfully?

options.text: The reservation status is updated to 'shipped'
The reservation record is deleted (for marketplace='shopify' and the matching orderId)
The reservation quantity is transferred to `TBL_PRODUCTS_MASTER.committed_stock`
Nothing — stock reservations are managed separately

questions.question: In `notifications-receiver.php`, what prevents duplicate processing of the same logistic status update for the same AWB?

options.text: A database unique constraint on `TBL_FULFILLMENT(trackingId, shipmentStatus)`
A file-based lock at `ROOT_PATH/tmp/logistic_update/{awb}-{status}.lock` using `createLock()`/`releaseLock()` in a `finally` block
An in-memory cache check via Redis
The `TBL_API_RUNTIME` table deduplication check

questions.question: Which of the following webhook events in `notifications-receiver.php` has NO HMAC signature verification?

options.text: `order_update` — Shopify order webhook
`refund_updates` — Razorpay payout webhook
`gokwik_settlement` — GoKwik/Easebuzz settlement data
`logistic_update` — logistics provider status update

questions.question: What does `respond_202_and_detach()` do in the `order_update` webhook handler?

options.text: Queues the request for later batch processing
Sends HTTP 202 "Accepted", flushes output, closes the client connection, then continues PHP execution in the background
Redirects the webhook to an async worker process
Stores the payload in `TBL_API_RUNTIME` and returns immediately

questions.question: In `update_returns_status()`, when a return is completed with `r_returnType='replace'`, what Shopify API call is made?

options.text: `shopify_dashboard->cancel_order()` on the original order
`shopify_dashboard->create_replacement_order_sp()` — creates a new order with 100% discount and "Replacement" tag
`shopify_dashboard->update_order_sp()` — updates the original order's tags
`shopify_dashboard->create_gift_card_sp()` — always issues a gift card

questions.question: When a COD order return is completed, how is the customer refunded?

options.text: Shopify issues the refund directly to the original payment method
A Razorpay PayoutLink is created via `$razorpay->PayoutLink->create()`, and the link is sent to the customer (refund record inserted into `TBL_SP_REFUNDS`)
A gift card is always issued via `create_gift_card_sp()`
The refund is processed through GoKwik settlement

questions.question: After a return is completed, what does `$stockist->update_inventory_status($uid, 'qc_pending')` accomplish?

options.text: Marks the UID as permanently returned and unavailable
Routes the returned UID back into the QAQC pipeline — the item will appear in QC scanning (`get_uid_details` from Day 9) for quality re-verification
Transfers the UID to a special returns warehouse location
Deletes the inventory record and creates a new one

questions.question: What differentiates `r_source='customer_return'` from `r_source='courier_return'` in `TBL_SP_RETURNS`?

options.text: `customer_return` is from Shopify's return API; `courier_return` is from a logistics partner's RTO (Return to Origin) when delivery fails
`customer_return` is for defective products; `courier_return` is for wrong products
`customer_return` requires approval; `courier_return` is automatic
`customer_return` gets refunded; `courier_return` gets replacement

questions.question: In the NDR tab query filter (`view_order_query`), what is the significance of the 9 AM daily cutoff?

options.text: NDR attempts are only processed before 9 AM
Shipments picked up before 9 AM yesterday (or today if after 9 AM) are filtered to show only "stale" NDR entries where delivery should have been attempted
Customer support hours start at 9 AM so NDR calls begin then
The logistic provider sends NDR webhooks at 9 AM daily

questions.question: In the Razorpay refund webhook flow, what is the lifecycle of `TBL_SP_PAYOUT_STATE`?

options.text: Created when order is placed, deleted when order is shipped

Created when `payout_link.processing` event fires (with `contactId` and `paymentLinkId`), used to link payout to refund record, then deleted after `payout.processed` event when UTR is written to `TBL_SP_REFUNDS`
Created on return received, deleted on return completed
Permanent record that tracks all payment states

questions.question: How many Razorpay integration touchpoints exist in the Shopify module, and where do they occur?

options.text: 2 — cancel_order refund and return refund only

4 — (1) cancel_order void/refund check, (2) return_completed COD PayoutLink creation, (3) refund_updates webhook for payout status tracking, (4) razorpay_payments webhook for payment capture/POS orders
1 — only the refund_updates webhook
3 — payment capture, refund creation, settlement import

questions.question: In the `ShopifyPayments` class, what does `getDifferenceDetails()` calculate?

options.text: Only the total refund amount for an order
The difference between expected payout (sale - PG fees - shipping - refunds - RTO - COD charges + claims) and actual settlement (logistic charges + remittance amounts)
The total shipping cost vs actual delivery cost
The Shopify commission on each order

questions.question: What is the class hierarchy of the Shopify connector system?

options.text: `shopify_dashboard` → `shopify` → `shopify_listner` (linear chain)
`shopify` is the base class; `shopify_dashboard` and `shopify_listner` both extend `shopify`; `ShopifyPayments` is a standalone class
All 4 classes are independent with no inheritance
`ShopifyPayments` extends `shopify_dashboard` which extends `shopify`
